title: ServerNotification
description:
Protocol-level notifications visible to all consumers.

43 base variants from cocode-rs design + 9 TS gap additions = 52 total. Each variant has an explicit `#[serde(rename = "...")]` for its wire method. See `event-system-design.md` Section 2.

oneOf.description: Session result (final usage, cost, stop reason).
Thread item updated (e.g. tool execution began).
Hook executed (post-completion summary; from cocode-rs base). Kept alongside HookStarted/Progress/Response to preserve the 43-variant base contract.
Hook execution progress (TS gap P1 — stdout/stderr streaming).
Output from a user-executed local command (REPL `!` prefix). Matches TS `SDKLocalCommandOutputMessage` (coreSchemas.ts:1590-1602).
Files persisted to disk (file upload/snapshot completion). Matches TS `SDKFilesPersistedEvent` (coreSchemas.ts:1672-1692).
MCP elicitation completed (form submission or cancellation). Matches TS `SDKElicitationCompleteMessage` (coreSchemas.ts:1779-1792).
Tool use summary from background haiku summarization. Matches TS `SDKToolUseSummaryMessage` (coreSchemas.ts:1769-1777).
Tool execution progress (bash/powershell long-running). Matches TS `SDKToolProgressMessage` (coreSchemas.ts:1648-1659). Sent at most once per 30 seconds per `parent_tool_use_id`.

ElicitationCompleteParams.description:
Matches TS `SDKElicitationCompleteMessage` (coreSchemas.ts:1779-1792).

Emitted after an MCP server's elicitation request is resolved (either submitted or cancelled).

FilesPersistedParams.description:
Matches TS `SDKFilesPersistedEvent` (coreSchemas.ts:1672-1692).

TS emits this when files are uploaded or persisted (e.g. after a successful `filesApi` operation).

HookExecutedParams.description: Params for the cocode-rs-base `hook/executed` notification. Simpler than `hook/started` → `hook/response` lifecycle: this is a single post-completion summary keeping the 43-variant base contract.

LocalCommandOutputParams.description:
Matches TS `SDKLocalCommandOutputMessage` (coreSchemas.ts:1590-1602).

TS emits this when the user runs a local bash command via the REPL `!` prefix (not a tool call). The `content` field is the command output; TS types it as the raw output structure (typically stdout/stderr).

McpServerInit.description: MCP server init entry (inline struct in TS).

PluginInit.description: Plugin init entry (inline struct in TS).

SessionResultParams.description: Matches TS `SDKResultMessageSchema` (coreSchemas.ts:1407-1451). TS has two subtype variants (success/error) unified here with `is_error` flag.

errors.description: Error variant: list of error strings.

num_api_calls.description: coco-rs extension: num_api_calls for observability (not in TS).

permission_denials.description: Permission denials accumulated during the session.

result.description: Success variant: the agent's final result text.

SessionStartedParams.description: Matches TS `SDKSystemMessageSchema` with subtype 'init' (coreSchemas.ts:1457-1494). Sent once at session startup; carries the full bootstrap context the SDK consumer needs to render a UI.

agents.description: Agent type names available for Agent tool spawning.

slash_commands.description: Slash commands available in this session.

version.description: Release version of the coco-rs binary.

oneOf.description: Turn completed, waiting for user input.
Waiting for user action (approval, question, elicitation).

TaskCompletedParams.description: Matches TS `SDKTaskNotificationMessage` (coreSchemas.ts:1694-1713). TS calls this `task/notification`; coco-rs uses `task/completed` as the wire method for brevity, but fields match TS exactly.

TaskProgressParams.description: Matches TS `SDKTaskProgressMessage` (coreSchemas.ts:1750-1767). In TS, `description` and `usage` are required; other fields optional. The `workflow_progress` field carries the streaming state of local_workflow tasks — a delta batch of workflow state changes.

TaskStartedParams.description: Matches TS `SDKTaskStartedMessage` (coreSchemas.ts:1715-1733). TS has `description` required and `task_type` optional.

ThreadItem.description:
Semantic representation of a conversation thread item. Produced by `StreamAccumulator` from `AgentStreamEvent` sequences. Used in `ServerNotification::ItemStarted / ItemUpdated / ItemCompleted`.

See `event-system-design.md` Section 1.6.

ThreadItemDetails.description:
Tool-specific semantic mapping.

Mapping rules (from `event-system-design.md` Section 6.2): - Bash → `CommandExecution` - Edit/Write → `FileChange` - WebSearch → `WebSearch` - mcp__* → `McpToolCall` - Agent/Task → `Subagent` - all others → `ToolCall` - text content → `AgentMessage` - thinking → `Reasoning` - errors → `Error`

oneOf.description: Bash tool → command execution with output.
Edit/Write tools → file change with diff info.
All other tools (Read, Glob, Grep, etc.).

TokenUsage.description: Per-request token counts (returned by LLM API).

ToolProgressParams.description:
Matches TS `SDKToolProgressMessage` (coreSchemas.ts:1648-1659).

Long-running tool progress (Bash, PowerShell). TS throttles emission to ≤1 per 30 seconds per `parent_tool_use_id`. coco-rs StreamAccumulator may emit this independently from `AgentStreamEvent::ToolUseStarted`.

ToolUseSummaryParams.description:
Matches TS `SDKToolUseSummaryMessage` (coreSchemas.ts:1769-1777).

Background Haiku-based summary of a batch of tool uses. TS uses this to compress verbose tool output before it's displayed or archived.
